response: The EU’s General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), effective May 2018, applies globally to any organization processing EU residents’ personal data and mandates rights such as access, erasure, portability, and explicit consent, plus breach reporting within 72 hours and fines up to 4 % of global revenue or €20 million. Notable penalties include a €1.2 billion fine on Meta (2023), €746 million on Amazon, and over €200 million on Google, illustrating the regulation’s enforcement teeth. GDPR has inspired similar privacy laws worldwide (e.g., California’s CCPA, Brazil’s LGPD, India’s DPDP Act) but faces criticism for imposing heavy compliance costs on small firms, generating user fatigue with cookie banners, and uneven enforcement across member states. Despite these challenges, it has cemented data privacy as a fundamental right and set the global benchmark for data‑protection legislation.
